page: Microsoft Azure DC4ds_v3 is a virtual machine instance belonging to the DDCSv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The configuration provides 4 physical cores and 4 vCPUs, with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio. Memory capacity is 32.0 GB, yielding a resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance includes 307 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not bundle any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations place the CPU and memory performance of the DC4ds_v3 consistently in the average range. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance is recorded at 1897.48, while the multi-core performance reaches 7566.05. Memory read-only bandwidth for small cached blocks is measured at 423.49 GB/sec, dropping to 51.12 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. The instance demonstrates average capabilities in software compilation, scoring 6928.0 on Geekbench Clang, and database operations, scoring 4002.29 on PassMark. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is a relative bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25% at 31.97 GB/sec. Similarly, while small 135M LLM inference is average, medium 7B LLM inference is weak, processing prompts at 17.58 tokens/sec and generating text at 7.23 tokens/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the DC4ds_v3 offers a balanced profile for memory-intensive workloads that do not require GPU acceleration. The 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio provides high memory density relative to the compute core count, making it suitable for caching layers, relational databases, and memory-bound enterprise applications. The inclusion of 307 GB of local SSD storage and Premium Storage compatibility enhances its utility for database operations. It is less suitable for heavy multi-threaded decompression or medium-to-large language model inference due to documented performance trade-offs in those areas.
description: Microsoft Azure DC4ds_v3 is a virtual machine in the DDCSv3 family, utilizing the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is equipped with a dedicated 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon 8370C processor featuring 4 physical cores and 4 vCPUs on an x86_64 architecture. The instance provides 32.0 GB of RAM, establishing an 8.0 GB per core memory density, alongside 307 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. Performance benchmarks generally fall in the average tier, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1646.0 and multi-core score of 5403.0. While it handles database operations and small-model LLM inference reliably, it exhibits performance trade-offs in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and 7B LLM inference. This server is best suited for memory-optimized workloads, relational databases, and caching applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DC4ds_v3 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB of RAM, and 307 GB of local storage, optimized for memory-intensive database workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon cores and local storage for database and caching workloads.

32.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per core memory density
